title: Two-Factor Authentication
drDigitalIntro: Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) adds a second security check on top of your password. After you enter your password, you also need to prove your identity a second way — usually a 6-digit code sent to your phone or generated by an app. Even if someone steals your password, they still can't get in without your phone. 2FA is one of the best ways to protect your accounts.

playgroundTask.question: You get a 2FA text message with the code 847293 — but you didn't try to log in. What should you do?

options.text: Enter the code right away before it expires
Ignore the code — someone may have tried to access your account
Share the code with someone who asks for it over the phone
Reply to the text with your password

drDigitalSuccess: Correct! If you get a 2FA code you didn't request, someone tried to log in as you. Never share the code — your bank will never ask for it.
drDigitalHint: A 2FA code that arrives when you're not logging in is a warning sign. What should you do with it?
